after_rl_output: A single queen bee can lay up to 2,000 eggs per day. The number of offspring she produces in her lifetime is estimated at around 100 million. This means that the total population of honeybees (Apis mellifera) is made up almost entirely of workers produced by just one or two queens. In fact, it's said that if you were to remove all but one female from an entire colony of honeybee colonies, there would be enough worker females left over to keep the colony going for several years. Bees are social insects and live in large colonies with complex societies. They communicate through chemical signals called pheromones. Honeybees have three castes: the queen, drones, and workers. The queen lays eggs while the drones mate with the queen. Workers perform various tasks such as foraging, caring for young, defending the hive, and producing wax. Worker bees typically live about six weeks during summer, although some may survive until winter. Drones die after mating, usually within a few days. Queen bees can live for up to five years, although their average lifespan is only about one year. The queen controls the reproductive cycle of the colony by laying eggs into cells containing food. She also regulates the amount of pheromone released by other bees, which helps control the behavior of the colony. The queen stores sperm from multiple males in specialized sacs near her ovaries. When she needs to fertilize an egg, she selects sperm from one of these males and uses them to fertilize the egg. The queen has no role in foraging, defense, or caring for young. Her main job is to reproduce. The queen is responsible for controlling the growth rate of the colony and regulating its reproduction. If the queen dies, the colony will eventually starve because the new queen won't know where to find food sources. The queen must always eat well so that she can store enough energy to make more eggs. The queen eats nectar and pollen directly from flowers, rather than consuming stored food like the rest of the colony members. The queen's diet consists mainly of royal jelly, which contains nutrients essential for development. Royal jelly is secreted by salivary glands located on the underside of the tongue. It is rich in proteins, vitamins, minerals, and amino acids. The queen consumes this substance daily, often storing excess amounts in special sacs near the ovary. after_rl_output: The Indian rupee is a multiple of 100, and its symbol is ₹. It has been used as the official currency since 1 January 1950. In India, it replaced the British pound sterling at par value on 15 April 1947, and the Indian rupee replaced the Indian farthing (which had itself replaced the Indian anna) on 1 April 1948. The Indian rupees are divided into paise or paisa, with one rupee equal to 100 paise. The Indian government announced that the country would switch from the existing monetary system based on pounds, shillings, and pence to a new system based on the rupee, which was already being widely accepted by traders and merchants. This change took effect on 15 August 1947. On 1 April, 1948, the Indian rupees were made legal tender for all transactions, replacing the Indian farthings. The Indian farthing was equivalent to 1/4th of an Indian anna. The Indian anna was equivalent to the old Indian silver rupee coin. The Indian silver rupees were equivalent to the British pound. The Indian pound was further subdivided into 20 shillings and each shilling into eight pence. The Indian pence was further subdivised into four annas. The Indian Anna was further subdivides into 16 farthings. A farthing was further subdivide into 12 annas. An anna was further subdivid into 16 paise. Paise was further subdivisde into 100 annas. One rupee was further subdivise into 100 paises. The Indian Rupee was introduced after independence in 1947 and was initially pegged at par value to the Pound Sterling. The Indian Government decided to introduce the new currency system on 15th August 1949.
